name: joomscan
title: joomscan

commands.name: joomscan

arguments.description: |   Use a proxy to connect to the target URL
arguments.raw: --proxy=PROXY                   |   Use a proxy to connect to the target URL

arguments.description: |   Output the current version and exit.
arguments.raw: --version                       |   Output the current version and exit.

Usage:	joomscan [options]

--url | -u <URL>                |   The Joomla URL/domain to scan.
--enumerate-components | -ec    |   Try to enumerate components.

--cookie <String>               |   Set cookie.
--user-agent | -a <User-Agent>  |   Use the specified User-Agent.
--random-agent | -r             |   Use a random User-Agent.
--timeout <Time-Out>            |   Set timeout.
--proxy=PROXY                   |   Use a proxy to connect to the target URL
           Proxy example: --proxy http://127.0.0.1:8080
                                  https://127.0.0.1:443
                                  socks://127.0.0.1:414

binary_metadata.file_type: Bourne-Again shell script, ASCII text executable

apt_metadata.description:
OWASP Joomla Vulnerability Scanner Project
This package contains JoomScan, short for [Joom]la Vulnerability [Scan]ner.
It's a project in perl programming language to detect Joomla CMS
vulnerabilities and analysis them.

use_cases: Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Indexing only — no automated invocation. The operator must manually inspect the repository before adding to a toolchain.
Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Pair with the LLM system-prompt stanza to ensure the chain planner knows how to call this tool.
